steps.title: Set up the problem
steps.body: A uniform rod $AB$ of length $4\,\text{m}$ and weight $50\,\text{N}$ is smoothly hinged to a wall at end $A$ and held horizontal by a vertical prop force $P$ applied at end $B$. Find $P$.

steps.title: Locate the centre of mass
steps.body: The rod is uniform, so its weight of $50\,\text{N}$ acts vertically downward at its midpoint — a distance $\dfrac{4}{2} = 2\,\text{m}$ from $A$ (and also $2\,\text{m}$ from $B$).

steps.title: Choose a pivot to eliminate the unknown hinge force
steps.body: The hinge at $A$ exerts an unknown reaction force on the rod. Taking moments **about $A$** makes that reaction's perpendicular distance zero, so it drops out of the equation — leaving only $P$ and the weight.

steps.title: Apply the principle of moments about A
steps.body:
Clockwise moment (the weight, at $2\,\text{m}$) must equal the anticlockwise moment (the prop force $P$, at $4\,\text{m}$):

$$
P \times 4 = 50 \times 2.
$$

steps.title: Solve for P
steps.body:
$$
P = \frac{50 \times 2}{4} = \frac{100}{4} = 25\,\text{N}.
$$

The prop must supply an upward force of $25\,\text{N}$ to hold the rod horizontal.
